title: passiflora × exoniensis
intro: Passiflora × exoniensis is a hybrid plant species of the passionflower genus, created by crossing Passiflora caerulea and Passiflora edulis. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including sedative and anti-anxiety effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Passiflora × exoniensis include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of menopause and irritable bowel syndrome. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or supplement, although more research is needed to fully explore its potential benefits and interactions.
